page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon E5-2667 v4 processor with an x86_64 architecture, delivering 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate average performance across several standard workloads, alongside notable limitations in compute-intensive tasks. The instance demonstrates average capabilities in single-threaded operations, including gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench single-core tests. However, stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance falls into the bottom 25%, as do Passmark CPU physics and encryption tests. Memory read bandwidth is average at 39.89 GB/sec for large blocks, but large block write bandwidth is weak at 23.56 GB/sec.

Given the older Intel Xeon E5 v4 architecture, this instance represents a legacy hardware profile. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that require dedicated CPU cores and moderate memory capacity without demanding cutting-edge compute speeds. Tradeoffs include weak multi-core scaling, poor performance on medium-sized LLM inference models, and weak database operations. Appropriate workloads include static web serving, Redis caching, basic software compilation, and general-purpose applications that do not require high-speed local storage or heavy cryptographic processing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2667 v4 cores running at 3.2 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this x86_64 server lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows average performance in single-threaded tasks, such as gzip compression and Geekbench single-core tests, alongside average memory read bandwidth. However, it exhibits weak multi-core CPU performance, weak database operations, and poor medium-model LLM inference speeds. This instance is best suited for workloads like static web serving, Redis caching, and basic software compilation where dedicated CPU allocation is required but extreme multi-core throughput is not critical.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2667 v4 cores, 32 GB RAM, and a 3 Gbps network baseline, offering balanced performance for standard workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.2xlarge is an 8-vCPU general-purpose server with 32 GB RAM, running on dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2667 v4 processors.
tagline: A dedicated eight-core x86_64 server with balanced memory allocation for standard web serving and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2667 v4 physical cores at 3.2 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Average performance in static web serving and Redis operations
Weak multi-core CPU scaling and database operation benchmarks
Suitable for basic software compilation and caching workloads
